mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change vs 20-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or RSAFS declines while SPX still rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, it signals tightening monetary policy that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS > 2.5% over at least 10 triggered instances, or t-test p > 0.05 for negative conditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined signal of PAYEMS rising and UNRATE falling fails to produce positive forward returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or pairwise rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, which precedes corporate earnings deterioration and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines >1% but average 60-day SPX return is positive over at least 5 such instances, or signal false positive rate >40%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1300K signal above-trend construction activity, reflecting strong economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return is not significantly positive when HOUST > 1300 (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising while consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating above 3% year-over-year, the Fed is forced into aggressive tightening that crushes equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS > prior month
failure_condition: Combined trigger condition occurs but average 20-day SPX return is positive, or rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate (below 4%) indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and pre-empts future Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations and results in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 4% is associated with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or rank IC < -0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth outpaces industrial production (INDPRO) growth over two consecutive quarters, demand is outstripping supply, creating inflationary pressures that lead the Fed to tighten policy and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > INDPRO growth over 2 quarters
failure_condition: GDPC1 outpaces INDPRO but average 60-day SPX return is positive over at least 3 distinct episodes, or signal IC < 0.05.
